response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack software engineer with strong web, backend, and some embedded/C/C++ experience, but the role requires deep specialization in SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, and AI-accelerator performance architecture. The resume shows no experience with deep-learning workload optimization, GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture, or system-on-chip performance modeling. The skill set is almost entirely orthogonal to a Principal Performance Architect role for AI hardware, making this a poor fit despite the candidate's overall seniority and software craftsmanship.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career with low-latency, high-throughput systems experience
Some C/C++ and embedded exposure (National Instruments LabWindows/CVI, robotic arms)
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware-software co-design experience
No AI accelerator, GPU architecture, or deep-learning optimization background
Lacks the specialized hardware/performance-architecture focus the principal IC role demands

key_factors.factor: AI accelerator / hardware architecture domain mismatch
No SoC/IP modeling or HW-SW co-design
Some C/C++ and embedded systems exposure
